title: lupinus washoensis
intro: Lupinus washoensis, also known as the Wasco lupine or Wasco bush lupine, is a species of flowering plant in the lupine family, native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Native American medicine, where its roots and leaves have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lupinus washoensis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Lupinus washoensis may include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
